situation: audit-2026-07-04-001 finding ES-M001: score_e3_external_stability() hardcoded return score=2 with no actual detection. External dependency changes invisible to route health scoring, making E3 dimension meaningless.
action: Replaced stub with real implementation that checks package.json/pyproject.toml/requirements.txt/Pipfile.lock/go.mod/Cargo.toml for HEAD~5..HEAD changes via git diff --name-only. Returns score=1 when deps changed, score=2 when stable, score=1+unavailable:true when dep files missing or git fails. No reward for unobservable state.
outcome_evidence: syntax OK via ast.parse; smoke test on autonomous-studio (no dep files) returns score=1+unavailable:true+checked_files=[]; smoke test on personal-assistant (has requirements.txt, no recent changes) returns score=2+checked_files=['requirements.txt']; opt-worktree.sh committed and pushed to origin/auto/opt-route-health-shift-1783100529
next_suggestion: 派生 fix #2: ES-M002 (E4 git diff failure defaults to full score — same pattern as E3, should return unavailable). Then ES-M003 (triage.py archive sequence race). After pending fixes clear, trigger next full audit.
